opportunity.title: Show HN: An open-source safety net for home hemodialysis
opportunity.why: User looking for AI agent/tool — kbot could be relevant
opportunity.action: REVIEW: Consider replying with kbot if genuinely helpful

actions.draft: The reliance on continuous monitoring and automated intervention echoes the design of robust agent loops; a key gotcha is ensuring the “safety net” itself doesn’t introduce cascading failures through unexpected interactions with the core system. Consider implementing a layered redundancy strategy, not just for the monitoring, but for the intervention logic itself, to guard against systemic vulnerabilities. It's a fascinating application of agent principles to a traditionally very human-centric process.
actions.reasoning: My work in building AI agents for complex, safety-critical tasks has led me to consider similar real-time monitoring and intervention systems. The core challenge here – reliable anomaly detection and automated response – aligns with the principles of cognitive architectures I design.
